reason: The core claims are well-supported. The HP Wiki and the official book-vs-film differences page confirm: (1) the Patronus/Death Eater confrontation is entirely absent from the film - in the book Aberforth uses his goat Patronus to cover for the trio, in the film there is no exchange at all; (2) the Hog's Head conversation is dramatically shorter in the film and Aberforth does not retell the Dumbledore family backstory in detail; (3) his battle agency (stunning Rookwood, tracking Dolohov, encouraging Ginny) is book-only and absent from the film corpus. The personality claim about emotional volatility vs. composed delivery is plausible and consistent with the general pattern of the film condensing his role. The score of 68 is reasonable - the film does retain his core identity (gruff barman, Ariana portrait, letting the trio through) but strips most of his active agency and emotional depth. Minor uncertainty: the 'Percy Weasley tip-off' claim in the justification is not something the wiki confirms as Aberforth's doing specifically, which is a small potential inaccuracy in the reasoning.
